It contributes to fulfil Danone&rsquo;s mission to bring health through food to as many people as possible, especially during the first 1000 days of life. We are currently present in more than 40+ African countries including Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;&nbsp;Human Resources HRBP - Operations

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos

About the Job


	To partner, with the operations functions (Engineering, Production, Quality, Supply Chain &amp; Logistics) to deliver their objectives through attracting, developing, nurturing, and retaining talent.


About You


	Relevant master&rsquo;s degree and at least 8-10 years of work experience in HR
	Strong knowledge in HR business partnering; employee relations, change management &amp; transformation and employment law
	&nbsp;Experience with HR Information Systems.

Human Resources HRBP - Operations Job at Danone Early Life Nutrition




        
    

        
        Danone Early Life Nutrition is one of Danone Group’s strategic business units (SBU) specialized in the field of infant nutrition. It contributes to fulfil Danone’s mission to bring health through food to as many people as possible, especially during the first 1000 days of life. We are currently present in more than 40+ African countries including Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:  Human Resources HRBP - Operations

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos

About the Job
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	To partner, with the operations functions (Engineering, Production, Quality, Supply Chain & Logistics) to deliver their objectives through attracting, developing, nurturing, and retaining talent.


About You


	Relevant master’s degree and at least 8-10 years of work experience in HR
	Strong knowledge in HR business partnering; employee relations, change management & transformation and employment law
	 Experience with HR Information Systems.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Field Risk & Internal Control Officer",
  "description": "Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors&rsquo; first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Risk &amp; Internal Control Officer (North East)

Job Location: Bauchi

Role Overview


	We are seeking a diligent and ethically grounded professional to serve as a key link between our risk oversight framework and field operations.
	This role is responsible for proactively identifying and investigating cases of misconduct&mdash;among Business Relationship Managers (BRMs), reviewing their performance data, and ensuring alignment with internal control standards.
	This is a hands-on role that requires a strong presence in the field, critical thinking, and the ability to distill field observations into actionable risk insights.


Key Responsibilities
Investigations &amp; Field Monitoring:


	Lead targeted investigations into suspected misconduct including double-dealing, agent impersonation, unauthorized onboarding, and unethical field behavior.
	Conduct unannounced field inspections and validation exercises to verify BRM claims, agent status, and compliance with policies.
	Interview field stakeholders (agents, merchants, supervisors) to gather intelligence and document findings in a structured, objective format.


Risk-Based Data Review:


	Analyze BRM activity reports and operational metrics to detect irregularities, risk exposures, or behavioral red flags.
	Correlate field data with internal dashboards and reporting systems to ensure integrity, accuracy, and completeness.
	Flag suspicious trends for escalation and further review by Risk, Compliance, or Internal Audit teams.


Internal Control Testing:


	Assess the effectiveness of controls guiding BRM activities and agent lifecycle processes (recruitment, management, offboarding).
	Recommend enhancements to close control gaps, prevent repeat incidents, and support sustainable field governance.
	Collaborate with process owners to roll out improved monitoring tools and corrective measures.


Reporting &amp; Escalation:


	Prepare concise, evidence-backed investigation reports, highlighting the root cause, impact, and recommended action steps.
	Maintain secure logs of field engagements, complaint resolutions, and closed investigations.
	Contribute to quarterly risk dashboards and field control assessments for management and board reporting.


Stakeholder Liaison:


	Serve as the first line contact for risk-related concerns from regional leads, area managers, and frontline employees.
	Build trust with BRMs while maintaining professional distance to ensure objectivity and ethical independence.
	Provide risk insights to performance, disciplinary, or compliance processes where applicable.


Candidate Profile
Qualifications:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Risk Management, Internal Control, Business Administration, Finance, Criminology, or a related field.
	Minimum 3 years of experience in internal control, field audit, risk monitoring, or investigative roles within financial services, fintech, or agent networks.


Skills and Attributes:


	Strong investigative mindset with excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
	High integrity, discretion, and professionalism in managing sensitive information.
	Field experience and comfort working with informal environments and diverse stakeholders.
	Proficiency in Excel and/or basic analytics tools (Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms is a plus).
	Excellent report writing and verbal communication skills.


Preferred Experience:


	Prior exposure to agent banking, POS distribution networks, or BRM performance frameworks.
	Experience managing whistleblowing complaints or conducting workplace misconduct investigations.
	Familiarity with regulatory requirements around internal control, risk management, and field operations.
	Ability to speak Hausa, Igbo or Yoruba.


What we can offer you


	Culture: We put our people first and prioritize the well-being of every team member. We&rsquo;ve built a company where all voices are heard, and we look out for one another. Above all, we are human.
	Learning: We have a learning- and development-focused environment with an emphasis on knowledge sharing and training.
	Compensation: You&rsquo;ll receive an attractive salary, pension, health insurance, and an annual bonus, plus other benefits.


What to expect in the hiring process


	A preliminary phone call with the recruiter.
	An interview with the hiring team.
	An interview with a member of our executive team.

Field Risk & Internal Control Officer Job at Moniepoint




        
    

        
        Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors’ first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Risk & Internal Control Officer (North East)

Job Location: Bauchi

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a diligent and ethically grounded professional to serve as a key link between our risk oversight framework and field operations.
	This role is responsible for proactively identifying and investigating cases of misconduct—among Business Relationship Managers (BRMs), reviewing their performance data, and ensuring alignment with internal control standards.
	This is a hands-on role that requires a strong presence in the field, critical thinking, and the ability to distill field observations into actionable risk insights.


Key Responsibilities
Investigations & Field Monitoring:


	Lead targeted investigations into suspected misconduct including double-dealing, agent impersonation, unauthorized onboarding, and unethical field behavior.
	Conduct unannounced field inspections and validation exercises to verify BRM claims, agent status, and compliance with policies.
	Interview field stakeholders (agents, merchants, supervisors) to gather intelligence and document findings in a structured, objective format.


Risk-Based Data Review:


	Analyze BRM activity reports and operational metrics to detect irregularities, risk exposures, or behavioral red flags.
	Correlate field data with internal dashboards and reporting systems to ensure integrity, accuracy, and completeness.
	Flag suspicious trends for escalation and further review by Risk, Compliance, or Internal Audit teams.


Internal Control Testing:


	Assess the effectiveness of controls guiding BRM activities and agent lifecycle processes (recruitment, management, offboarding).
	Recommend enhancements to close control gaps, prevent repeat incidents, and support sustainable field governance.
	Collaborate with process owners to roll out improved monitoring tools and corrective measures.


Reporting & Escalation:


	Prepare concise, evidence-backed investigation reports, highlighting the root cause, impact, and recommended action steps.
	Maintain secure logs of field engagements, complaint resolutions, and closed investigations.
	Contribute to quarterly risk dashboards and field control assessments for management and board reporting.


Stakeholder Liaison:


	Serve as the first line contact for risk-related concerns from regional leads, area managers, and frontline employees.
	Build trust with BRMs while maintaining professional distance to ensure objectivity and ethical independence.
	Provide risk insights to performance, disciplinary, or compliance processes where applicable.


Candidate Profile
Qualifications:


	Bachelor’s degree in Risk Management, Internal Control, Business Administration, Finance, Criminology, or a related field.
	Minimum 3 years of experience in internal control, field audit, risk monitoring, or investigative roles within financial services, fintech, or agent networks.


Skills and Attributes:


	Strong investigative mindset with excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
	High integrity, discretion, and professionalism in managing sensitive information.
	Field experience and comfort working with informal environments and diverse stakeholders.
	Proficiency in Excel and/or basic analytics tools (Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms is a plus).
	Excellent report writing and verbal communication skills.


Preferred Experience:


	Prior exposure to agent banking, POS distribution networks, or BRM performance frameworks.
	Experience managing whistleblowing complaints or conducting workplace misconduct investigations.
	Familiarity with regulatory requirements around internal control, risk management, and field operations.
	Ability to speak Hausa, Igbo or Yoruba.


What we can offer you


	Culture: We put our people first and prioritize the well-being of every team member. We’ve built a company where all voices are heard, and we look out for one another. Above all, we are human.
	Learning: We have a learning- and development-focused environment with an emphasis on knowledge sharing and training.
	Compensation: You’ll receive an attractive salary, pension, health insurance, and an annual bonus, plus other benefits.


What to expect in the hiring process


	A preliminary phone call with the recruiter.
	An interview with the hiring team.
	An interview with a member of our executive team.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Sales Executive",
  "description": "JMG is a highly diversified electro-mechanical solution provider, offering a wide-ranging portfolio that includes power generation, Solar power, Electrical infrastructures, Air compressors, Vertical transportation, and HVAC Cooling solutions. With over 25 years of experience in Africa and strong partnerships with world-renowned brands such as FG Wilson, Legrand, ABB, Kaeser Compressors, Trane, TK Elevator, LONGi, Deye, Must, Livfast, Lithion, and EAE Electric, JMG combines global technologies with regional expertise to meet the development needs of the African market.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Sales Executive

Job Location: Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a skilled and experienced Snr. Sales Executive to join our sales team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving sales growth, managing key client accounts, identifying new business opportunities, and leading strategic sales initiatives to achieve organizational revenue targets.
	This role is critical to ensuring business expansion, customer retention, and sustained profitability through effective sales and relationship management.


Qualifications


	Minimum of SSCE / O-Level Certificate.
	5-7 years of proven experience as a Snr. Sales Executive, specifically working in business development, strategic sales, and client relationship management.
	A bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Marketing, Business Administration, Sales, or a related field is an added advantage.
	Experience with sales planning, negotiation, and customer relationship management practices commonly used in structured business environments.


Minimum Requirements:


	Develop and implement strategic sales plans to achieve assigned revenue and business growth targets.
	Identify, pursue, and secure new business opportunities while maintaining strong relationships with existing clients.
	Conduct sales presentations, negotiations, and contract discussions in line with company objectives and customer requirements.
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and customer feedback to support effective sales planning and decision-making.
	Prepare periodic sales reports, forecasts, and performance analyses to support management reporting and business strategy.
	Coordinate with internal teams to ensure efficient service delivery and maintain high levels of customer satisfaction.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should:&nbsp;Click here to apply online
Or
Send their CV to: career@jmglimited.com using the job title and the preferred Location as the subject of the mail.",

Senior Sales Executive Job at JMG




        
    

        
        JMG is a highly diversified electro-mechanical solution provider, offering a wide-ranging portfolio that includes power generation, Solar power, Electrical infrastructures, Air compressors, Vertical transportation, and HVAC Cooling solutions. With over 25 years of experience in Africa and strong partnerships with world-renowned brands such as FG Wilson, Legrand, ABB, Kaeser Compressors, Trane, TK Elevator, LONGi, Deye, Must, Livfast, Lithion, and EAE Electric, JMG combines global technologies with regional expertise to meet the development needs of the African market.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Sales Executive

Job Location: Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Description


	We are seeking a skilled and experienced Snr. Sales Executive to join our sales team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving sales growth, managing key client accounts, identifying new business opportunities, and leading strategic sales initiatives to achieve organizational revenue targets.
	This role is critical to ensuring business expansion, customer retention, and sustained profitability through effective sales and relationship management.


Qualifications


	Minimum of SSCE / O-Level Certificate.
	5-7 years of proven experience as a Snr. Sales Executive, specifically working in business development, strategic sales, and client relationship management.
	A bachelor’s Degree or HND in Marketing, Business Administration, Sales, or a related field is an added advantage.
	Experience with sales planning, negotiation, and customer relationship management practices commonly used in structured business environments.


Minimum Requirements:


	Develop and implement strategic sales plans to achieve assigned revenue and business growth targets.
	Identify, pursue, and secure new business opportunities while maintaining strong relationships with existing clients.
	Conduct sales presentations, negotiations, and contract discussions in line with company objectives and customer requirements.
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and customer feedback to support effective sales planning and decision-making.
	Prepare periodic sales reports, forecasts, and performance analyses to support management reporting and business strategy.
	Coordinate with internal teams to ensure efficient service delivery and maintain high levels of customer satisfaction.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should: Click here to apply online
Or
Send their CV to: [email protected] using the job title and the preferred Location as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Zonal Manager (POS Agent Support)",
  "description": "PalmPay is an Africa-focused fintech firm, launched in 2019. PalmPay is a mobile payments platform that offers a number of financial services.


We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Zonal Manager (POS Agent Support)
Job Location: Lagos
Department: Sales



Role Overview
Contractor shall work as a Zonal Manager to carry out the following responsibilities:


	The ZM makes decisions through company policies to promote its business standard in line with work ethics.
	ZM assigns daily tasks to RMs and ensures the Job is done in line with our business policies.
	ZM also oversees the RMs and other business operations within the cities and ensures all walk-in agents are attended to.
	ZM works with other departments like IV/warehouse and sales teams to ensure all pos under after-sales are stored back in the warehouse and maintain compliance with our business rules.
	Manage assigned projects and program components to deliver services in accordance with established objectives.


Key Qualifications
Education &amp; Experience:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration or any other related discipline.&nbsp;&nbsp;
	A minimum of 6 years of experience in the Fintech Industry.
	Experience as a POS Support is an added advantage.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong facilitation, coaching, and presentation skills.
	Ability to assess training needs and translate them into impactful learning programs.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and stakeholder management skills.
	Strong project management and organizational abilities.



&nbsp;",

  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

  },
  "jobLocation": {

       "postalCode": ". ",

       "streetAddress": ". ",

Zonal Manager (POS Agent Support) Job at PalmPay




        
    

        
        PalmPay is an Africa-focused fintech firm, launched in 2019. PalmPay is a mobile payments platform that offers a number of financial services.


We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Zonal Manager (POS Agent Support)
Job Location: Lagos
Department: Sales
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    





Role Overview
Contractor shall work as a Zonal Manager to carry out the following responsibilities:


	The ZM makes decisions through company policies to promote its business standard in line with work ethics.
	ZM assigns daily tasks to RMs and ensures the Job is done in line with our business policies.
	ZM also oversees the RMs and other business operations within the cities and ensures all walk-in agents are attended to.
	ZM works with other departments like IV/warehouse and sales teams to ensure all pos under after-sales are stored back in the warehouse and maintain compliance with our business rules.
	Manage assigned projects and program components to deliver services in accordance with established objectives.


Key Qualifications
Education & Experience:


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration or any other related discipline.  
	A minimum of 6 years of experience in the Fintech Industry.
	Experience as a POS Support is an added advantage.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong facilitation, coaching, and presentation skills.
	Ability to assess training needs and translate them into impactful learning programs.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and stakeholder management skills.
	Strong project management and organizational abilities.
